meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing and overcome version control issues. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your app.
content.answer_block: To solve the chaos of unversioned code while implementing SaaS pricing, leverage Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated commits. By treating your pricing logic as a versioned state machine, you ensure that every feature deployment is traceable, reversible, and monetizable from your very first student project.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder of 'final_v2_real_final' files, paralyzed by the fear that one wrong keystroke will destroy weeks of progress. This is the 'no version control' trap—the silent career-killer for budding developers. You aren't just building an app; you are building a professional foundation.

We are going to pivot your workflow. By integrating robust SaaS pricing models early, you force yourself to structure code logically, making version control not just a chore, but a natural byproduct of your development process. Let's turn your student project into a production-ready SaaS.

steps.title: Initialize Versioned Foundation
steps.description: Establish a Git repository immediately to track your pricing logic evolution, preventing code drift.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate project structure for a SaaS pricing module, ensuring all files are ready for Git initialization.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && claude commit -m 'Initial project scaffolding'

steps.title: Define Pricing Schema
steps.description: Create a configuration file to store tier logic, allowing you to version your pricing strategy separately from your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model including Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create pricing_config.json with defined tiers and feature access levels'

steps.title: Automate Commits via Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to document every change to your billing logic, ensuring a clear audit trail for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that monitors pricing_config.json for changes and suggests meaningful git commit messages.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit 'Update pricing tiers to include student discount logic'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Connect your pricing logic to your app features so that users only access what they pay for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a decorator function that checks the current user's pricing tier against requested feature requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement middleware for tier-based feature access control'

steps.title: Verify Deployment Integrity
steps.description: Run final checks to ensure your version history accurately reflects the current state of your production pricing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a summary report of all changes made to the pricing module since the project inception.

pro_tips: Always commit your pricing logic in a separate branch before testing new discount codes.
Treat your configuration files as code; never hardcode pricing values directly into your UI components.
Use git tags to mark production releases of your pricing model so you can revert instantly if billing errors occur.
Document the 'Why' behind every pricing change in your commit history to explain your business logic to future employers.
Automate your testing suite to run every time you modify the pricing schema to prevent accidental access leaks.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your pricing model is versioned and stable, integrate a payment gateway like Stripe and deploy your first live environment to Vercel or Railway.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical discipline to outpace 90% of your peers. Stop fearing your own files and start building with professional-grade rigor. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
